Hey guys, JB here. These weekend motivations are really about just giving you cool things to think about food for thought. Have a great day and let's do some serious butt kicking on Monday. All right? Take care. When you're selling something, don't be concerned that what your cost is to justify what you charge something. It doesn't matter if it cost, my straight line calls zero. It's information. The reason people pay for this is because they make money from it, the benefit to them. Remember, always say to yourself, you got to walk in your potential customer shoes. You deal with business owners, right? Think about what is the value of what you're selling? What does it mean to them in real terms? Customers, how many more customers they've got from it? What's each customer worth? It's like in the mortgage business. The way you close a mortgage loan, right? You're very, very, a lot of your mortgage mortgage, right? You want to close a refinance deal, right? Someone's on a 30-year mortgage and you want to take them down to a 15-year mortgage, right? Well, the most powerful equation you could run is called life of loan savings. You do the equation. Well, okay, so if I take you down to a 15-year mortgage at a lower rate, well, your payment might go up to be $300 a month, but you'll take 10 years off your mortgage, 10 years times $2,000 and you get it before you can't do a life of loan savings. And the reason that's so powerful is you always project a lifetime value of what you're doing. Don't say, you know, we're going to bring you 10 customers and then you're bringing 10 customers. What's the lifetime value of that? And then what you do is when you explain your service, we do this for you. We provide these five services. This first service can get you about 10 extra customers a month. This service provides you an extra 8 to 10 customers. This one, 9 to 12, you know, that toll it up. So at the end of the day, all each of these services combined will yield some between 100 and 150 new customers a month. Now, before you said your average cost is worth $2,000, right? Do the math. The program's worth $300,000 if you. We charge you $12,000 in monthly payments. How could you say no to that? And there's a money back guarantee. But instead of that, they were doing is here's the charge of $2,000. Let me explain why we have to have 300 Indians make your website. You understand it's insane. It doesn't matter what something costs you. Nobody cares. What they want to know is one of the benefits to them. And in this case, specifically money, life value. Remember the ultimate equation in many businesses is what is the lifetime value of a customer? What's the lifetime value? Lots of companies, they go out there and they lose money on the opening transaction and they make a fortune over the long term because they sell the customer again, again, again. So they have a marketing expense to bring the customer in and then a sales expense to actually close them. But once they have that customer, there you go. Remember that when you're constructing your pitches and always the ultimate logical equation, logical certainty is do the math. 50 new customers a year. Each customer's worth $2,000. That's a hundred thousand of extra revenue a year. The program course you $500 a month. Talk about a no brainer. That's a logical case. And plus over the long term, what I could do for you in the way of that's your emotional case. Imagine yourself three years down the road with all these new customers, your business is booming, you're opening the second location. Believe me. Give me one shot. You'll be very, very impressed. Get it guys? Don't forget your mathematics. Run your equations. What's a customer worth over the life? What are you charging for your service? How many customers are you getting? It's so simple.
